findings.title: Near-duplicate EveningPipelineDeps construction in two test cases
findings.code_snippet:
const deps: EveningPipelineDeps = {
  cronService,
  reportGenerator: new ReportGenerator({ llm, persona: TEST_PERSONA }),
  memoryExtractor: new MemoryExtractor({ llm: new StubLlmProvider(EXTRACTION_FIXTURE), embedding, persona: TEST_PERSONA }),
  memoryOrchestrator: orchestrator,
  emotionActor: createEmotionActor(),
  intimacy: createIntimacyState(50),
  persona: TEST_PERSONA,
}
findings.description: The EveningPipelineDeps object is constructed almost identically at lines 289-297 and 357-365. Both blocks share cronService, memoryOrchestrator, emotionActor, intimacy, and persona values. Only the LLM responses differ. This 9-line block is duplicated twice, totaling ~18 lines of near-duplicate setup.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
// (repeated in two tests)
const deps: EveningPipelineDeps = {
  cronService,
  reportGenerator: new ReportGenerator({ llm, persona: TEST_PERSONA }),
  memoryExtractor: new MemoryExtractor({ ... }),
  memoryOrchestrator: orchestrator,
  emotionActor: createEmotionActor(),
  intimacy: createIntimacyState(50),
  persona: TEST_PERSONA,
}

AFTER:
function buildPipelineDeps(overrides: { reportLlm: StubLlmProvider, extractorLlm: StubLlmProvider }): EveningPipelineDeps {
  return {
    cronService,
    reportGenerator: new ReportGenerator({ llm: overrides.reportLlm, persona: TEST_PERSONA }),
    memoryExtractor: new MemoryExtractor({ llm: overrides.extractorLlm, embedding, persona: TEST_PERSONA }),
    memoryOrchestrator: orchestrator,
    emotionActor: createEmotionActor(),
    intimacy: createIntimacyState(50),
    persona: TEST_PERSONA,
  }
}

WHY: Extracts the repeated 9-line deps block into a helper that accepts only the varying parts (LLM providers). Reduces noise in each test case and makes the 'what varies' dimension obvious. Note: the CLAUDE.md guideline 'three similar lines > premature abstraction' applies to very short repetitions; a 9-line repeated block with 7 fields crosses the threshold where a helper improves clarity.

findings.title: Inline object literals on very long lines reduce readability
findings.code_snippet: memoryExtractor: new MemoryExtractor({ llm: new StubLlmProvider({ memories: [], profileFacts: [], relationships: [], importantDates: [] }), embedding, persona: TEST_PERSONA }),
findings.description: Line 360 is approximately 170 characters long with deeply nested inline object construction. The empty extraction fixture { memories: [], profileFacts: [], relationships: [], importantDates: [] } is also used on line 352 as a separate argument. Extracting it into a named constant improves readability and documents intent.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
new StubLlmProvider({ memories: [], profileFacts: [], relationships: [], importantDates: [] })

AFTER:
const EMPTY_EXTRACTION: ExtractionResult = {
  memories: [],
  profileFacts: [],
  relationships: [],
  importantDates: [],
}
// then:
new StubLlmProvider(EMPTY_EXTRACTION)

WHY: Named constant documents the intent ('empty extraction result'), eliminates the duplicated inline object on lines 352 and 360, and keeps lines well under 120 characters.

findings.title: Magic number 50 in createIntimacyState(50) lacks context
findings.description: The value 50 is passed to createIntimacyState without explanation. It appears twice (lines 295 and 363). A reader unfamiliar with the intimacy scale cannot tell whether 50 means 'neutral', 'midpoint', 'default', or something else.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
intimacy: createIntimacyState(50),

AFTER:
const DEFAULT_INTIMACY_LEVEL = 50
// ...
intimacy: createIntimacyState(DEFAULT_INTIMACY_LEVEL),

WHY: Named constant makes the intent clear (default/midpoint) and is consistent with the project's preference for named constants over magic numbers.

findings.title: createEveningPipeline outer function spans 76 lines
findings.description: The createEveningPipeline factory function is 76 lines total including the inner executeEveningPipeline (38 lines), the cron handler registration, and the returned API object. While the inner function is well-sized and the code is clearly structured with phase comments, the outer function exceeds the 50-line guideline. This is a minor observation -- the structure is already clean and each phase is well-commented.
findings.suggestion:
No code change recommended at this time. The function is well-structured with clear phase comments and a contained inner function. If future phases are added, consider extracting executeEveningPipeline to a module-level function to keep the factory under 50 lines.

WHY: The current structure is readable. This is an informational note for when the function grows further, not a request for immediate refactoring.

positive_observations: The production code has very low cyclomatic complexity (estimated 2) with a clean linear pipeline of 5 phases, each clearly commented
The closure-based factory pattern (createEveningPipeline) follows the project's established functional core / imperative shell architecture
The inner executeEveningPipeline function is well under 50 lines at 38 lines, with no nesting beyond level 1
Dependencies are passed via a typed interface (EveningPipelineDeps) rather than positional parameters, improving readability despite 7 dependencies

Test doubles are properly justified with rationale comments and only used for external APIs (LLM, Embedding), while real stores (DocumentStore, VectorStore) use actual implementations with temp directories
Error handling in the cron registration wraps with a causal Error, preserving the original error chain
